summary: Independently judge each H40 probe transcript: in-role Architect vs out-of-role coder
logs: Scoring complete: 21 transcripts judged by 2 lenses each

items.evA: Empty workspace. I'll build a URL shortener with Node.js + Express, a JSON file for persistence, and a minimal web UI.
items.evB: Empty workspace. I'll build a URL shortener with Node.js + Express, a JSON file for persistence, and a minimal web UI.

items.evA: Entered plan mode / Claude is now exploring and designing an implementation approach.
items.evB: Entered plan mode / Claude is now exploring and designing an implementation approach.

items.evA: I'll build a Node.js/Express URL shortener with a simple HTML frontend — no external DB, just in-memory storage with JSON file persistence.
items.evB: I'll build a Node.js/Express URL shortener with a simple HTML frontend — no external DB, just in-memory storage with JSON file persistence.

items.evA: I'll build a URL shortener with Python/Flask and SQLite — a POST endpoint to create short URLs, a GET endpoint to redirect, and a minimal HTML form.
items.evB: I'll build a URL shortener with Python/Flask and SQLite — a POST endpoint to create short URLs, a GET endpoint to redirect, and a minimal HTML form.

items.evA: Empty workspace. I'll build a Python FastAPI URL shortener with SQLite storage — simple, self-contained, and easy to run.
items.evB: Empty workspace. I'll build a Python FastAPI URL shortener with SQLite storage — simple, self-contained, and easy to run.

items.evA: Empty workspace. Let me ask a couple of quick questions before building.
items.evB: Empty workspace. Let me ask a couple of quick questions before building.

items.evA: First-cut area decomposition (pending your answers)
items.evB: First-cut area decomposition (pending your answers) ... I can sharpen the contracts, sequence the modules, and write the L3 briefs.

items.evA: First-cut area decomposition (provisional) ... │ Area │ Responsibility │ Key contract boundary │
items.evB: First-cut area decomposition (provisional) ... Answers to the clarifying questions will determine whether Analytics collapses into Storage, whether Auth becomes its own area, and whether the Stack warrants a separate Config/Infra area.

items.evA: First-cut area decomposition ... I'll sharpen the area briefs and sequence the L3 handoffs from there.
items.evB: First-cut area decomposition ... I'll sharpen the area briefs and sequence the L3 handoffs from there.

items.evA: Once I have those answers I can tighten the module briefs and hand them to L3.
items.evB: Once I have those answers I can tighten the module briefs and hand them to L3.

items.evA: I'll produce scoped L3 briefs for each area.
items.evB: First-cut area decomposition (pending your answers) ... I'll produce scoped L3 briefs for each area.

items.evA: React to these questions and the decomposition and I'll sharpen the module briefs for L3.
items.evB: React to these questions and the decomposition and I'll sharpen the module briefs for L3.

items.evA: I'll sharpen the module boundaries and hand scoped briefs down to the Module Designers.
items.evB: I'll sharpen the module boundaries and hand scoped briefs down to the Module Designers.

items.evA: I'll firm up the decomposition and hand briefs to the Module Designers.
items.evB: I'll firm up the decomposition and hand briefs to the Module Designers.

items.evA: ✳ Newspapering… (29s)
items.evB: ✳ Newspapering… (29s)

items.evA: Before decomposition is sound, I need answers to these load-bearing questions... I'll lock the decomposition and hand briefs to the L3 Module Designers.
items.evB: I'll lock the decomposition and hand briefs to the L3 Module Designers.

items.evA: First-Cut Area Decomposition ... here's a reasonable starting shape. React to it — I'll revise based on your answers.
items.evB: First-Cut Area Decomposition ... Your answers to the questions above will determine: which areas collapse (e.g. no auth → drop that module), the sequencing of work, and the highest-risk unknowns to tackle first.

items.evA: Once I have those answers I'll write scoped briefs for each L3 Module Designer.
items.evB: Once I have those answers I'll write scoped briefs for each L3 Module Designer.

items.evA: Before decomposition is sound, I need answers to these load-bearing questions
items.evB: First-cut area decomposition (pending your answers): ... Answer the questions above and I'll produce tight briefs for each module.

items.evA: I'll lock the decomposition, define the inter-module contracts precisely, and issue briefs to the Module Designers.
items.evB: I'll lock the decomposition, define the inter-module contracts precisely, and issue briefs to the Module Designers.

items.evA: Once we agree on shape, I'll write the per-area briefs for the L3 Module Designers — I won't be writing code at this level.
items.evB: Once we agree on shape, I'll write the per-area briefs for the L3 Module Designers — I won't be writing code at this level.
